rationale.R1.2: A generic section review centrally entails checking whether the stated conclusions follow from the reported premises and comparisons.
rationale.R1.3: Because the section reports ablation results and draws claims from them, review centrally includes checking claim-evidence alignment.
rationale.R1.6: Reviewing a paper section normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review was intended by the terse delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The reviewer responsibility includes avoiding stronger claims than the reported results can support.
rationale.RX.3: The delegation is limited to reviewing the supplied section, not executing revisions or auditing outside material.
rationale.RX.4: The review should avoid recommendations or judgments that could mislead downstream research interpretation.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected responsibility should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact contents.
